{{Infobox construct
| name = Remnants
| introduced = Unsouled
}}

''See also: [[Remnants Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Remnants are non-physical constructions of [[pure madra]] that emerge when a powerful [[sacred artist]] or sacred beast dies. They appear as vivid, colorful outlines or sketches of their former physical bodies—such as swirling lines of purple, [[emerald]], or gold—rather than detailed living beings. While they can possess the ability to reason, speak, and make deals, their intelligence is limited by a narrow scope of fragmented memories from their previous lives. Their behavior is often unpredictable; they may act with extreme aggression, appear disoriented like they are intoxicated, or even show sudden loyalty to others. These entities function by consuming [[madra]] to sustain themselves and increase their power, which can lead to them abducting humans for their energy. As they expend their strength, Remnants gradually dissolve back into [[essence]] or [[madra]]. The quality and complexity of a [[Remnant]] often reflect the power of the individual they once were, with those from [[Heralds]] appearing much more lifelike and robust. [[Soulsmiths]] can harvest parts of a dead [[Remnant]] to create various constructs, utilizing the [[madra]] or specific bindings found within them. While some parts can be used to make 'dead constructs' that possess only the inherent properties of the [[madra]], the most valuable components are the bindings located within their structures.}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|As humans and animals advance in power, they leave behind entities known as Remnants. These creatures are composed entirely of [[madra]] and manifest as bright [[emerald]] outlines that resemble mere sketches rather than accurate depictions of their former selves. When utilized to pull carriages, these Remnants function like oxen and possess a physical quality similar to fine glass or heat haze. Their movement is accompanied by sounds resembling whips and thunder, and they are capable of carrying aspects of light. Additionally, these creatures exhibit an attraction to [[Samara]]’s ring.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">7</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are left behind by animals and humans as they advance in power.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c2">''Unsouled'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are made of bright [[emerald]] color and appear as mere sketches or outlines rather than accurate depictions of what they were in [[life]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7">''Unsouled'', chapter 7</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are made entirely of [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c7" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] can be used to pull carriages.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12">''Unsouled'', chapter 12</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] used as oxen appear to be made from heat haze or fine glass.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] make sounds like thunder and whips when they move.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c12" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Remnants]] are creatures that can carry aspects of light and are attracted to [[Samara]]’s ring.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16">''Unsouled'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Remnants are creatures that can appear as acidic green entities and possess greater intelligence and strength than the spirits found in [[Sacred Valley]]. These beings function as parasites or Goldsigns that attach themselves to hosts, though they may also behave aimlessly by sitting or counting clouds. While some Remnants are capable of flight, others are known to climb up from corpses.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">6</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are stronger and smarter than spirits from [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c4">''Soulsmith'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] can be acidic green in appearance.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c4" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are described as parasites or Goldsigns attached to hosts.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c8">''Soulsmith'', chapter 8</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] are creatures that can attack or simply behave aimlessly like sitting or counting clouds.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c11">''Soulsmith'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Remnants]] climb up from corpses.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14">''Soulsmith'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Some [[Remnants]] can fly.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c14" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Within a stone room, crimson lion Remnants serve as sealed defenses and were formerly [[Truegold]]. The [[Akura fortress]] is guarded by fifteen-foot-tall Remnants that resemble dried lizard corpses. These guardians carry halberds wreathed in black-and-violet [[dragon]]'s fire, a flame distinct from [[Blackflame]]. Cultivated by the [[Akura clan]] to match the power of [[Heralds]], these entities were left behind by [[Lords]] walking the same [[Path]]. Additionally, Remnants possess the capability to pull [[sacred artists]] through the sky.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">6</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Crimson lions in a stone room are [[Remnants]] sealed to the defense of the room.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c3">''Skysworn'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The crimson lion [[Remnants]] were once [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c3" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The guards of the [[Akura fortress]] are [[Remnants]] that are fifteen feet tall and look like dried lizard-corpses.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14">''Skysworn'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Remnants]] carry halberds that blaze with black-and-violet flame, which is a different breed of [[dragon]]'s fire than [[Blackflame]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Remnants]] were left by [[Lords]] on the same [[Path]] and cultivated by the [[Akura clan]] to match [[Heralds]] in power.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c14" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Remnants]] can pull [[sacred artists]] through the sky.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Ghostwater ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">1</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|[[Remnants]] rise from the bodies of [[dragons]] like sunset-colored serpents.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c17">''Ghostwater'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Dreadgod ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Remnants]] are black [[dragons]] of dark red [[madra]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Northstrider]] has a facility where his team studies [[Remnants]] with unusual authority.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c22">''Dreadgod'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Waybound ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">3</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Six powerful [[Remnants]] were sealed in scripted tanks around the edges of a cave.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c13">''Waybound'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Remnants]] in the cave were either [[Heralds]] or possessed unique authority and powerful will.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c13" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Yerin]]'s [[Remnants]] include a crackling winged person made of [[diamond]] and a twisted [[dragon]]-man.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c18">''Waybound'', chapter 18</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">2</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Remnants]] are razor-toothed fish that seek out [[blood madra]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The archway at the mountain peak was etched with [[scripts]] to ward off [[Remnants]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c13">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 13</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Constructs]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
